_comment: Pinned sqlite3 command-line tools for the opt-in installer used by the
The sha256 values below were computed from bytes actually downloaded from
sqlite.org on the pinned_date. They are not transcribed from anywhere and
are not guesses. If a download ever fails to match, that is a hard failure:
the artifact is deleted and the installer exits non-zero. Never 'fix' a
mismatch by updating the hash to whatever arrived -- that converts the one
check standing between a maintainer and a substituted binary into a
sqlite.org publishes SHA3-256 on its download page rather than SHA-256.
Neither Get-FileHash nor a baseline POSIX toolchain computes SHA3 without
extra dependencies, so we pin SHA-256 computed locally. To refresh: download
each URL, verify the upstream SHA3-256 on https://sqlite.org/download.html by
hand, then record the local SHA-256 here along with a new pinned_date.
min_version is 3.33.0 because the scripts require '.param set' (3.32.0, for
bound parameters -- string-interpolated SQL over hostnames and usernames is
both an injection vector and a plain bug for any user named O'Brien) and

darwin-arm64._note: sqlite.org publishes no arm64 macOS build (verified 404 on 2026-07-23). Apple Silicon uses the x64 build under Rosetta 2. If Rosetta is absent the installer fails and the package-manager path is the answer.

package_manager_hint.linux: sudo apt install sqlite3   (or dnf/pacman equivalent)
